metadata.title: Base performance review rubric
metadata.description: Default severity rules for /gaia-performance-review covering N+1 queries, bundle size, caching, algorithmic complexity, and memory management. Layer 1 of the four-layer rubric composition.

severity_rules.pattern: Code iterates over a collection and issues one query per element (loop-driven query, lazy-load inside a `for`/`forEach`/list comprehension over an ORM relation).
severity_rules.description: N+1 query patterns scale linearly with collection size and are the leading cause of latency regressions on real datasets.
severity_rules.remediation: Batch the queries via eager loading (`include`/`joinedload`/`Prefetch`) or fetch the related rows in a single bulk query.

severity_rules.pattern: GraphQL resolver fetches related entities one-by-one without DataLoader-style batching across the request.
severity_rules.description: Per-field resolver fetches multiply request fan-out and amplify database load under nested queries.
severity_rules.remediation: Wrap the per-entity fetch in a request-scoped DataLoader so duplicate keys collapse into one batch.

severity_rules.pattern: Front-end bundle imports a heavy dependency (moment.js, lodash, full icon library) for a single helper instead of using a tree-shakable subset or modern equivalent.
severity_rules.description: Heavy dependencies inflate first-load JS budget and degrade Core Web Vitals on real-user traffic.
severity_rules.remediation: Switch to a tree-shakable alternative (date-fns, lodash-es with named imports) or replace with a focused helper.

severity_rules.pattern: Single-route component imports the entire feature graph synchronously instead of lazy-loading via dynamic `import()` or React.lazy / Vue defineAsyncComponent.
severity_rules.description: Eager-loaded routes inflate the critical-path bundle and delay Time-to-Interactive on slow connections.
severity_rules.remediation: Code-split per route; lazy-load secondary panels and modals so they pay their bytes only when used.

severity_rules.pattern: Endpoint recomputes an idempotent, expensive result on every request (heavy aggregation, third-party fetch) without any caching layer.
severity_rules.description: Recomputing idempotent expensive work on every request wastes capacity and degrades p99 latency.
severity_rules.remediation: Cache at the appropriate tier (HTTP `Cache-Control`, Redis, in-process LRU); set TTL aligned with staleness tolerance.

severity_rules.pattern: Cached value is invalidated on every write rather than on the writes that actually change the underlying data — cache thrash.
severity_rules.description: Over-eager invalidation defeats the cache: hit-rate collapses to near zero and the cache becomes pure overhead.
severity_rules.remediation: Invalidate only on writes that mutate the cached aggregate; consider write-through or read-through patterns.

severity_rules.pattern: Static asset is served without long-lived `Cache-Control: max-age` and content-hashed filename — repeat visits re-download unchanged bytes.
severity_rules.description: Missing far-future cache headers force the browser to revalidate static assets on every visit.
severity_rules.remediation: Hash asset filenames and serve with `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable`.

severity_rules.pattern: Nested loop pair operates on user-supplied collections without a complexity guard — O(n²) over inputs that can grow unboundedly in production.
severity_rules.description: O(n²) over user-controlled inputs is a denial-of-service primitive: a 10x growth in input size becomes a 100x cost.
severity_rules.remediation: Replace with a hash-set / hash-map lookup (O(n)), or chunk the input with a hard cap and pagination.

severity_rules.pattern: User-supplied regular expression or expression with catastrophic-backtracking patterns is evaluated against attacker-controlled input.
severity_rules.description: Catastrophic-backtracking regexes (ReDoS) lock a worker thread for seconds on small inputs.
severity_rules.remediation: Validate or compile-check the pattern; use a linear-time regex engine (RE2) or set a hard execution timeout.

severity_rules.pattern: Long-lived data structure (cache, buffer, queue) accumulates entries without an eviction policy — unbounded growth.
severity_rules.description: Unbounded in-memory growth leads to OOM crashes once the workload exceeds the test envelope.
severity_rules.remediation: Add a size or TTL bound (LRU cache, ring buffer, queue with capacity) and surface eviction metrics.

severity_rules.pattern: Code reads a large blob (file, HTTP response, database column) into memory in one shot when streaming would suffice.
severity_rules.description: Whole-blob reads multiply RSS by request concurrency and are a leading cause of OOM under fan-out.
severity_rules.remediation: Stream the source — Node `Readable`, Python iterator, or chunked HTTP — and process in fixed-size windows.
